subgroups.title: Color lights
subgroups.description: Learn the three colors, test yourself, then practice with a quick mini game.

modules.title: Intro: red, amber, and green
modules.description: A vertical signal shows red at the top, amber in the middle, and green at the bottom. Learn what each color tells you before you drive or cross.

modules.title: MCQ: color lights
modules.description: Questions focused on red, amber, and green and where to stop.

modules.title: Mini game: match the light
modules.description: Six rounds — tap Stop, Slow / prepare, or Go for the color shown.

subgroups.title: Traffic lights and signals
subgroups.description: Study and quiz on steady lights, arrows, crossings, and following directions.

modules.title: Study: traffic lights and signals
modules.description:
Steady red: stop behind the stop line and wait. Do not cross unless directed by an authorised person.

Steady amber after green: stop unless it would be unsafe to stop; if you are already crossing the junction, clear it with care.

Steady green: go only if your way ahead is clear and you can do so safely; still give way where required (e.g. to pedestrians already crossing).

Red and amber together: prepare to go, but remain stopped until you see green.

Green arrow with a red light for straight ahead: you may follow the direction of the arrow when it is safe, as that movement is signalled for you.

Flashing amber at some crossings: usually means give way to pedestrians on the crossing; approach slowly and be ready to stop.

If a police officer or traffic warden gives a different signal from the lights, always follow the person directing traffic.

modules.title: MCQ: traffic and signals
modules.description: Lights, phases, and behaviour at signal-controlled junctions.
